title: Bridge Essay: Dose-response relationships of psilocybin-induced subjective experiences in humans.
concept: Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.
**Phase context:** Psychedelic decoupling — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states of consciousness.

**Dose-response relationships of psilocybin-induced subjective experiences in humans.** — J Psychopharmacol (2021). DOI: 10.1177/0269881121992676. PMID: 33663259. BACKGROUND: Psilocybin is the psychoactive component in Psilocybe mushrooms ('magic mushrooms'). Whether and how the quality of the psilocybin-induced experience might mediate beneficial health outcomes is currently under investigation, for example, in therapeutic applications. However, to date, no meta-analysis has investigated the dose-dependency of subjective experiences across available studies. AIM: Establishing dose-response relationships of the subjective experiences induced by psilocybin

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 14 (Nonordinary Rendering). Evaluation: Excellent (score=3.7)
